attempts.cap_hypothesis: Interleaved dual-cross-product x87 scheduling ceiling. The original computes edge_cross and pt_cross interleaved on the FPU stack — recomputing the shared (v1-v0) subtractions as two separate pairs (0x8e-9b, 0x9e-a8) and chaining ST0 via fld %st(2)/fld %st(4)/fsubrp/fmulp, then spilling edge_cross to [ebp+0x20] and reloading it three times for the vs-0 compares and the fdivs. Clean C89 emits two sequential cross-product computations instead. Branch skeleton (test 0x44 ==0, test 0x5 <0, test 0x41 final fcomps), -1 init via shared or hmtBc1,%eax, and mixed int/float output stores all match; no FPU operand-order warning; near-equal instruction counts (167 vs 164). This is the documented x87 ST0-chaining/interleaved-FPU cap, not a correctness bug.

attempts.reason: promoted 67.1->96.0: NOT a scheduling ceiling. Levers: edge-vector/point diffs as explicit ST-resident locals (ex/ey/cx/cy); volatile edge_cross (spilled once, reloaded 3x: ==0 test, divide, sign test); outer branch spelled edge_cross != 0.0f (divide branch = if-body, parallel = else); inner branch (edge_cross<0.0f) != side (enter = if-body, leave = else, je-to-leave layout); guards pt_cross > out[0] / enter > leave mirror FCOM order; edge_index=first_edge moved before out-record init. NaN paths verified against ref masks (0x44/0x5/0x41). Residual 4 insns = VC71 point-diff load reorder + FLD-const vs FCOMP-const operand choice.
